pages.title: "Webstudio Light and Dark Theme Switcher – Open Colors Pallete"

meta.description: "Brent’s long-form work spans action, drama, historical narrative, sci-fi, and documentary storytelling — from intimate character pieces to large-scale international productions. His lighting approach integrates effortlessly with camera, grip, and production teams, supporting cinematographers with clarity, adaptability, and seamless execution across Main, Rigging, 2nd Unit, and VFX Unit work."

"Brent brings speed, precision, and adaptable problem-solving to agency-driven commercial work. Trusted by leading global brands across automotive, fashion, luxury, technology, lifestyle, and beverage, he delivers lighting that supports fast creative decisions, tight schedules, and the visual identity of each campaign."

"Brent Sauls is an African Gaffer with nearly two decades of experience across features, series, and premium commercial work. Classically trained, he has worked every role in the lighting department — from trainee, spark, and best boy to Gaffer on Main Unit, Rigging, 2nd Unit, and VFX Unit — building a deep, lived understanding of both the craft and the crews behind it, a lighting technician shaped by the full spectrum of the discipline."

props.value:
<!-- Dark Theme CSS (Craft)-->
<style>
html[website-theme="dark"], .dark-theme {

--foreground-primary: var(--gray-1);
--foreground-secondary: var(--gray-6);
--foreground-accent: var(--indigo-1);
--foreground-muted: var(--gray-8);
--foreground-border: var(--gray-9);
--foreground-tertiary: var(--yellow-12);
--foreground-quaternary: var(--red-2);

--background-primary: var(--black);
--background-secondary: var(--gray-9);
--background-accent: var(--camo-3);
--background-card: var(--indigo-12);  
--background-tertiary: var(--yellow-8);
--background-quaternary: var(--red-10);
  
--focus-color:var(--indigo-10);

}

button[website-theme].active {
  background: var(--background-accent);
  color: var(--foreground-accent);
}
</style>

props.name: code
props.value:
<script>
// Determine theme and apply it
(function () {
  const root = document.documentElement;
  const saved = localStorage.getItem('website-theme');
  const prefersDark = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
  const theme = saved === 'dark' || saved === 'light' ? saved : 'auto';

  const resolvedTheme = theme === 'dark' ? 'dark' :
                        theme === 'light' ? 'light' :
                        prefersDark ? 'dark' : 'light';

  root.setAttribute('website-theme', resolvedTheme);

  if (!saved) localStorage.setItem('website-theme', 'auto');
})();

// Listen to OS theme changes
window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').addEventListener('change', e => {
  const saved = localStorage.getItem('website-theme');
  if (saved === 'auto' || !saved) {
    const newTheme = e.matches ? 'dark' : 'light';
    document.documentElement.setAttribute('website-theme', newTheme);
    if (typeof updateSwitcher === 'function') updateSwitcher(newTheme);
  }
});
</script>

props.name: code
props.value:
<script>
  (function () {
  const root = document.documentElement;
  const themesCycle = ['light', 'dark', 'auto'];

  // Apply theme globally and dispatch event for sync
  function applyTheme(theme) {
    localStorage.setItem('website-theme', theme);
    root.setAttribute('website-theme', theme);
    const prefersDark = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
    root.classList.toggle('dark-theme', theme === 'dark' || (theme === 'auto' && prefersDark));
    document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('themeChanged', { detail: theme }));
  }

  // Full switcher logic
  function applyFullTheme(theme, buttons) {
    localStorage.setItem('website-theme', theme);
    root.setAttribute('website-theme', theme);
    const prefersDark = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
    root.classList.toggle('dark-theme', theme === 'dark' || (theme === 'auto' && prefersDark));
    buttons.forEach(btn => {
      btn.classList.toggle('active', btn.getAttribute('website-theme') === theme);
    });
  }

  // Initialize theme switchers inside a container
  function initSwitchers(container) {
    const buttons = container.querySelectorAll('button[website-theme]');
    if (!buttons.length) return;

    const isShort = container.hasAttribute('website-theme-buttons');
    let currentTheme = localStorage.getItem('website-theme') || 'auto';

    if (isShort) {
      // Short switcher: only active button visible
      function renderShortButton(theme) {
        buttons.forEach(btn => {
          btn.style.display = btn.getAttribute('website-theme') === theme ? '' : 'none';
          btn.classList.remove('active');
        });
      }
      renderShortButton(currentTheme);

      buttons.forEach(btn => {
        btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
          const currentIndex = themesCycle.indexOf(currentTheme);
          const nextIndex = (currentIndex + 1) % themesCycle.length;
          currentTheme = themesCycle[nextIndex];
          applyTheme(currentTheme);
          renderShortButton(currentTheme);
        });
      });

      document.addEventListener('themeChanged', e => {
        currentTheme = e.detail;
        renderShortButton(currentTheme);
      });

      applyTheme(currentTheme);

    } else {
      // Full switcher: all buttons visible, active highlighted
      buttons.forEach(button => {
        button.addEventListener('click', () => {
          const selectedTheme = button.getAttribute('website-theme');
          applyTheme(selectedTheme);
          applyFullTheme(selectedTheme, buttons);
        });
      });

      document.addEventListener('themeChanged', e => {
        applyFullTheme(e.detail, buttons);
      });

      applyFullTheme(currentTheme, buttons);
    }
  }

  // Initial scan
  document.querySelectorAll('div,section,nav,header,footer').forEach(initSwitchers);

  // MutationObserver to handle dynamically added switchers (e.g. Radix Tabs)
  const observer = new MutationObserver(mutations => {
    mutations.forEach(mutation => {
      mutation.addedNodes.forEach(node => {
        if (!(node instanceof HTMLElement)) return;
        if (node.matches('div,section,nav,header,footer')) {
          initSwitchers(node);
        }
        node.querySelectorAll?.('div,section,nav,header,footer').forEach(initSwitchers);
      });
    });
  });
  observer.observe(document.body, { childList: true, subtree: true });

  // Sync theme between tabs/windows
  window.addEventListener('storage', e => {
    if (e.key === 'website-theme') {
      document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('themeChanged', { detail: e.newValue }));
    }
  });
})();
</script>

props.name: code
props.value:
<style>
html[website-theme="dark"] .logo-dark {
  display: block;
}

html[website-theme="dark"] .logo-light {
  display: none;
}

html[website-theme="light"] .logo-dark {
  display: none;
}

html[website-theme="light"] .logo-light {
  display: block;
}

/* AUTO MODE */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  html[website-theme="auto"] .logo-dark {
    display: block;
  }

  html[website-theme="auto"] .logo-light {
    display: none;
  }
}

@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) {
  html[website-theme="auto"] .logo-dark {
    display: none;
  }

  html[website-theme="auto"] .logo-light {
    display: block;
  }
}
</style>

children.value: Open Props includes Open Color, an open-source color scheme optimized for UI development.

Brent’s long-form work spans action, drama, historical narrative, sci-fi, and documentary storytelling — from intimate character pieces to large-scale international productions. His lighting approach integrates effortlessly with camera, grip, and production teams, supporting cinematographers with clarity, adaptability, and seamless execution across Main, Rigging, 2nd Unit, and VFX Unit work.
Selected work demonstrating Brent’s depth across Main Unit, Rigging, 2nd Unit, VFX Unit, and Additional Photography.
Focus: large-scale set electrics, continuity-driven lighting, and integration with complex studio workflows.
Our Girl (BBC, 2017–2018) — Rigging Gaffer (shot in Nepal)
Focus: heavy rigging, safe large-set installations, efficient pre-light systems, and multi-department coordination across stunt, action, and VFX-heavy environments.
Origins: The Story of Mankind (2012) — 2nd Unit Gaffer
Resident Evil: The Final Chapter (2016) — 2nd Unit Best Boy
Focus: action sequences, continuity with main-unit lighting, and rapid on-the-day problem solving.
The Empty Man (2020) — VFX Unit Gaffer
Focus: interactive lighting, plate lighting, controlled color environments, and integration with digital extensions.
Focus: maintaining continuity for reshoots and supplementary photography; matching established visual language.
Focus: grounding in discipline, technical fundamentals, and exposure to international lighting standards.

Brent brings speed, precision, and adaptable problem-solving to agency-driven commercial work. Trusted by leading global brands across automotive, fashion, luxury, technology, lifestyle, and beverage, he delivers lighting that supports fast creative decisions, tight schedules, and the visual identity of each campaign.

Brent Sauls is an African Gaffer with nearly two decades of experience across features, series, and premium commercial work. Classically trained, he has worked every role in the lighting department — from trainee, spark, and best boy to Gaffer on Main Unit, Rigging, 2nd Unit, and VFX Unit — building a deep, lived understanding of both the craft and the crews behind it, a lighting technician shaped by the full spectrum of the discipline.

He built his foundation on features such as Dredd 3D, Death Race 2, and Safe House, learning under seasoned lighting teams and absorbing the pace and discipline of international filmmaking. By 2014, he gaffered his first feature, Tiger House, establishing the path that would define his career.

As he continued leading smaller units, Brent broadened his craft as a technician on larger-scale features including Star Trek: Beyond, The Mummy, and Mowgli. Through this progression, Brent’s path led him to Gaffer African Queens, a project that earned a 2023 Emmy nomination for Best Cinematography.

He has grown through every major evolution in lighting — from tungsten to LED, from analogue dimmers to wireless control, and into modern Virtual Production environments. He has worked across multiple African countries, as well as Dubai and Nepal, adapting his craft to diverse crews, conditions, and storytelling traditions. His work blends technical fluency with creative responsiveness, supporting cinematographers with mood, structure, efficient workflow, reliability, and calm under pressure.

In commercial work, he is trusted by leading global brands across automotive, fashion, luxury, lifestyle, and technology — bringing speed, precision, and adaptable problem-solving to agency-driven environments.
Through Magic Arms Lighting, Brent extends this craft with world-class gear, rigging expertise, and a philosophy rooted in adaptability, evolution, and adding value to every set.
Experience that lives in the light — and learns from the shadows.
Driven by passion. Grounded in craft.

I love working with people who care deeply about what they do — and helping them bring their vision to life through light. Every frame is shaped through discipline, teamwork, and trust — because truth lives in contrast.
